join_order.build: num(uom=pure, value IS NOT NULL) → GROUP BY (adsh_code, tag_code) → MAX(value)
join_order.notes: Phase 1: single-threaded zone-map-guided scan builds one global max_map directly. No thread-local maps. No merge step. ~118k rows, 2 qualifying blocks — parallelism overhead (merge cost 12ms) exceeds benefit (saves ~1ms in build). Phase 2: single-threaded probe of max_map + fy direct-array lookup.
adsh_code == sub row_id by encoding contract; check sub/fy.bin[adsh_code] == (int16_t)2022 as O(1) direct array index. ~21% selectivity reduces survivors to ~24.8k rows.

filters.notes: pure_code resolved at runtime from shared/uom.dict; expected value 2 but MUST load from dict
applied as direct_array lookup sub/fy.bin[adsh_code] during Phase 2 probe

scan.access_pattern: zone_map_skip: only ~2 qualifying blocks scanned (~200k rows)

data_structures.uom_dict: load shared/uom.dict via load_binary_dict (tiny: 201 entries, 1.6KB); scan entries to find 'pure' code; store as pure_code (int8_t)
data_structures.tag_raw_dict: mmap shared/tag_numpre.dict (9.7MB, 198,311 entries); parse header once to build uint32_t offsets[198312] + uint16_t lengths[198311] arrays (single malloc each); string access = pointer into mmap buffer; NO per-string heap allocation; used only in output sort comparator and CSV decode
data_structures.name_raw_dict: mmap sub/name.dict (233KB, 86,135 entries); same offset+length approach; single malloc for offsets array; used only in output sort comparator and CSV decode
data_structures.m_hash_agg: single global compact_hash_map, capacity=262144 (next_pow2(118000*2)), allocated with malloc+memset (avoids mmap page-fault overhead); key=uint64_t packed(adsh_code<<32|tag_code); value=double max_value; empty sentinel key=UINT64_MAX; built single-threaded in Phase 1 directly — no thread-local maps, no merge phase
data_structures.sub_fy_array: mmap sub/fy.bin as int16_t[86135]; O(1) lookup by adsh_code
data_structures.sub_name_array: mmap sub/name.bin as int32_t[86135]; lookup name_code at probe time for survivors only
data_structures.output_candidates: std::vector<Candidate> collected single-threaded in Phase 2; partial_sort to get top 100

parallelism.notes: With only ~2 qualifying blocks (~118k qualifying rows), OMP parallelism overhead (thread wakeup, thread-local map alloc, merge) costs 12ms+ while saving <2ms in scan time. Run both Phase 1 and Phase 2 single-threaded. This eliminates: 2x mmap(4MB) page-fault overhead for thread maps, the 12ms merge loop, and OMP thread synchronization overhead.

indexes.notes: Read indexes/num_zonemaps.bin; per-block check uom_min <= pure_code <= uom_max; skip block if pure_code out of range. Reduces 395 blocks to ~2 qualifying blocks. Same logic as iter_0.

decode_at_output: s.name: sub_name_array[adsh_code] -> int32 name_code -> name_raw_dict pointer+length
sort comparator uses std::string_view (pointer+length) for name and tag comparison — no extra allocation

optimization_notes: Root cause is two Category-A plan inefficiencies: (1) data_loading wastes ~12-15ms on per-string heap allocations while loading tag_numpre.dict (198,311 entries, 9.7MB) using fread+vector<string> — each of the ~198k entries with average length ~47 bytes (above SSO threshold) triggers a separate heap allocation; fix is to mmap the dict file and build a uint32_t offsets array + uint16_t lengths array (2 mallocs total), with string data accessed directly as char* pointers into the mmap buffer; (2) the dim_filter/merge phase wastes 12ms because building 2 thread-local hash maps (4MB each) and then merging them costs more than it saves — with only ~2 qualifying blocks and ~118k pure rows, single-threaded Phase 1 takes ~3ms and eliminates the 12ms merge entirely, also removing mmap(MAP_ANONYMOUS) page-fault overhead for the thread-local maps. Expected new total: ~30-35ms vs 58ms current.
